name: planning
description: Ten-skill pipeline that turns a vague idea into executed work, keeps each project's contracts honest, and gives a cross-project portfolio view. brainstorming validates the design via question-driven exploration; planning-projects produces a staged plan with phase gates and Red-Green cycles (reconciling backlog and workflow specs at research time); executing-plans drives the plan with stage-gate checks, a default independent-evaluator pass at gates and close-out, stage-boundary handoff notes for context resets, opens/closes backlog entries, and audits workflow specs at close-out — now with a git-bootstrap preflight (inits a repo when missing), run-to-completion execution that doesn't pause between green stages, a platform stage-verify hook at each gate (e.g. android-dev's android-stage-verify), a two-tier white-box code review wired into execution (per-task quick review where a Critical blocks within the cycle budget, plus a per-stage deep review at the gate) via git-github's read-only code-reviewer agent, and SemVer version bumps across every mirror at close-out; applying-design-handoff redesigns an app to precisely reproduce a Claude Design handoff pack (tokens, components, layout, assets), auto-detecting a local exported bundle or a live claude.ai design-system project via the DesignSync tool, inventorying the app and its workflow specs, and reshaping functionality to fit the design — the design wins, but every behavior change is gated through workflow-spec (Changes/Removes WF-*) with user sign-off — then delegating cross-platform code to the ui-* agents and the sonnet-pinned design-handoff-reproducer subagent (per-slice precise reproduction) before a separate-evaluator fidelity-rubric verify loop; executing-plans drives it for a design-handoff/redesign task and fires that loop as a stage-gate hook; dispatching-parallel-agents fans out independent tasks marked Parallel YES and routes each to a stack-matched subagent (rust-expert, ui-android, testing-expert, …) via a shared stack-routing reference that executing-plans also uses to delegate independent, output-heavy sequential tasks off the orchestrator's context — guarded by a CI-enforced validate-stack-routing.py check that fails when the routing table names an agent/skill the marketplace no longer ships; no-fafo-debugging fires at the start of any debugging/diagnostic work to enforce evidence-first root-cause analysis, and gathers that evidence autonomously (reads logs, runs read-only diagnostics, reproduces failures, builds interceptors/probes itself) so the user is pulled in only when genuinely blocked; backlog owns the deferred-work register at docs/backlog.md (now with `unify` and `complete` subcommands); workflow-spec owns the behavior contracts at docs/workflows/ used to detect regressions after change; project-maturity scaffolds and audits a per-project docs/MATURITY.md across six publishing-readiness axes (Documentation, Security, Packaging, UI/UX, i18n, Testing & CI); portfolio is a cross-project orchestrator that scans ~/dev/, unifies plans+backlogs per project, rolls up a global backlog index and a global maturity dashboard at ~/.claude/. Skills hand off to each other and were designed as a unit.
